title: IT control testing sample evidence retrieval by control ID

description: Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "IT control testing sample evidence retrieval by control ID" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX testing attestation.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOX ITGC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes summary and sourcetypes sox:control_evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=summary`.
visualization: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory

knownFalsePositives: Changes during scheduled month/quarter end, controlled refactoring, or audit-monitored access.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=summary sourcetype=sox:control_evidence earliest=-365d control_id="ITGC-ACCESS-01"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.29"
| stats latest(evidence_hash) as h, count by control_id, test_period
```

Understanding this SPL

**IT control testing sample evidence retrieval by control ID (SOX testing)** — Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "IT control testing sample evidence retrieval by control ID" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX testing attestation.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: summary; **sourcetype**: sox:control_evidence. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=summary, sourcetype=sox:control_evidence, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by control_id, test_period** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ERP audit trail (SAP, Oracle ERP), reconcile to ITSM or change records where needed, and confirm results, field extractions, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
grandmaExplanation: We align the evidence for iT control testing sample evidence retrieval by control ID the way our SOX and audit work expects, using the data feeds the control owners already have. SOX is about making sure financial systems stay trustworthy for reporting.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
